heading: Arlington National Cemetery: other administrative matters
label: 2410A Arlington National Cemetery: other administrative matters
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–154, title VI, § 602(c)126 Stat. 1200Aug. 6, 2012“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to all interments at Arlington National Cemetery after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 2410A of title 38Aug. 6, 2012“(2).—ExceptionSubsection (b) of such section, as so added, shall not apply with respect to the interment of an individual for whom a request for a reserved gravesite was approved by the Secretary of the Army before .”January 1, 1962

L. 112–154, title VI, § 602(c)126 Stat. 1200Aug. 6, 2012“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to all interments at Arlington National Cemetery after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 2410A of title 38Aug. 6, 2012“(2).—ExceptionSubsection (b) of such section, as so added, shall not apply with respect to the interment of an individual for whom a request for a reserved gravesite was approved by the Secretary of the Army before .”January 1, 1962

children.text: Not more than one gravesite may be provided at Arlington National Cemetery to a veteran or member of the Armed Forces who is eligible for interment or inurnment at such cemetery.
The Secretary of the Army may waive the prohibition in paragraph (1) as the Secretary of the Army considers appropriate.
A gravesite at Arlington National Cemetery may not be reserved for an individual before the death of such individual.

children.label: A
children.text: The President may waive the prohibition in paragraph (1) as the President considers appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs and the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives.
